DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-13-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via ZOOM Video-conferencing:
Attorneys Caasey Hultin (Email: Casey.hultin@dolanlawfirm.com) and Katelyn Dembowski of the law offices of DOLAN LAW FIRM, PC (Tel: 415.421.2800) appear as counsels for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Emily M. Westfall (Email: ewestfall@mgmlaw.com) of the law offices of MANNING GROSS + MASSENBURG LLP (Tel: 415.512.4381) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

(State Bar No. 314769) 201 Spear Street, Suite 1800 San Francisco, California 94105 Fax: (415) 622-6791 Email: bkarren@mgmlaw.com

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides on record as reflected in the notes of the Certified Shorthand Reporter.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT UNITED BRANDS PRODUCT DESIGN DEVELOPMENT AND Marketing, Inc.'s demurrer to the complaint is overruled.

As a threshold matter, Defendant's attempt to designate itself as "specially appearing" is ineffective. Defendant has not asserted any jurisdictional objections; accordingly, by filing the demurrer, it has made a general appearance. (See Code Civ. Proc. sec. 1014 [a defendant appears in an action when the defendant answers, demurs, or files a notice of motion to strike]; id. sec. 418.10(e)(3) [failure to move to quash service of summons for lack of personal jurisdiction at the time of filing a demurrer constitutes a waiver of the issue of lack of personal jurisdiction]; Roy v. Superior Court (2005) 127 Cal.App.4th 337, 344 ["it is clear that a defendant who demurs or moves to strike must concurrently move to quash or dismiss, or any jurisdictional defect is waived."].)

The Court liberally construes the complaint with a view to substantial justice between the parties. (See Code Civ. Proc. sec. 452; Stevens v. Superior Court (1999) 75 Cal.App.4th 594, 601.) What is important is that the complaint as a whole contains sufficient facts to apprise the defendant of the basis upon which the plaintiff is seeking relief. (See Youngman v. Nevada Irrigation Dist. (1969) 70 Cal.2d 240, 245; Semole v. Sansoucie (1972) 28 Cal.App.3d 714, 719.) Further, a "demurrer for uncertainty is strictly construed, even where a complaint is in some respects uncertain, because ambiguities can be clarified under modern discovery procedures." (Khoury v. Maly's of California (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 612, 616; see also Lickiss v. Financial Industry Regulatory Authority (2012) 208 Cal.App.4th 1125, 1135 ["demurrers for uncertainty are disfavored, and are granted only if the pleading is so incomprehensible that a defendant cannot reasonably respond."].)

Here, contrary to Defendant's argument, the Complaint pleads sufficient ultimate facts that defendant intends the misuse of its nitrous oxide canisters and/or reasonably can foresee that misuse but fails to warn against it. (See, e.g., Complaint, 2:17-19 [defendant's knowledge of drug use], 2:23-25 [provocative packaging], 4:15-18 [agency/joint venture allegations with retailer], 5:16-17 [defendant's knowledge that customers intend to inhale nitrous oxide and failure to warn that inhalation is unsafe], 5:26-6:3 [sold without sufficient warning].) The Complaint sufficiently apprises defendant of the theories of liability and issues to be met. Defendant raises factual issues that cannot be resolved on demurrer, but that may be addressed through discovery and a subsequent speaking motion.

Written order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
